title: Compare Bank Accounts
summary: Use a bank account to keep money safe and track what you have.
learnerPromise: Use a bank account to keep money safe and track what you have.
whyItMatters: A bank account helps you keep money safe, receive payments, and track what you have.
realLifeUses: Receive your paycheck via direct deposit instead of cash.
Pay rent and bills online without visiting an office.
Keep your savings separate from spending money so you do not accidentally spend it.
Build a banking history that helps you get loans or credit later.
youWillLearn: Choose between a checking account and a savings account.
Open an account and understand the terms and fees.
Monitor your balance and read a bank statement.
miniChallenge: Compare two checking or savings accounts and write down one fee each may charge.
steps: Research banks or credit unions near you. Compare fees and minimum balance requirements.
Choose a checking account for daily spending and a savings account for goals.
Gather your ID and proof of address. Visit a branch or apply online.
Set up online banking so you can check your balance and transfer money.
Make your first deposit. Set a reminder to check your account weekly.
completionCriteria: You have an active bank account and know how to check your balance.
You can perform basic transactions — deposit, withdraw, and transfer — on your own.
commonProblems: Monthly fees eat into your balance — choose a fee-free account or learn how to waive fees.
You forget to check your balance and overdraft — set up low-balance alerts.
You do not understand a charge on your statement — call your bank and ask, that is what they are for.
tips: Credit unions often have better rates and fewer fees than big banks.
Never share your PIN or online banking password with anyone.
Set up account alerts for withdrawals, low balance, and deposits.
